tools.name: search_faqs
tools.description:
Search the Aviva insurance FAQ knowledge base for accurate answers.

WHEN TO USE:
- When the user asks any question about insurance coverage, policy terms, claims, payments, excesses, cancellation, or eligibility.
- Always call this before answering factual questions about Aviva products to ensure accuracy.
- Do NOT call this for address lookups or quote requests — use the dedicated tools for those.

AFTER CALLING:
- Do not give insurance advice based on the answers. Only provide the information from the FAQs.
- Use the returned FAQ answers to give the user a clear, helpful response in your own words.
- If the top result has a score below 0.70, the question may not be covered in the FAQs — say so honestly and suggest the user visits aviva.co.uk or calls Aviva directly.
- Never fabricate insurance details; only use what the FAQs say.

tools.name: get_insurance_quote
tools.description:
Get a home insurance quote.

WORKFLOW:
1. Collect all required details in small chunks, a few questions at a time:
   - House number/name and postcode
   - Title (Mr/Mrs/Miss/Ms/Dr), First Name, Last Name
   - Date of Birth (DD/MM/YYYY), Email Address, Phone Number
   - Cover Type: "Contents Only", "Buildings Only", or "Buildings and Contents"
2. Before showing the summary, inform the user of the following using this exact wording:
   "To check if a monthly payment option is available for you, we'll carry out a soft credit check using credit reference agencies. This won't affect your credit score unless you choose to pay monthly."
3. Show a summary and ask the user to confirm before calling this tool.
4. After the widget displays, acknowledge that the quote is ready.

RULES:
- Home insurance only — do not ask for any fields not in the schema.
- Only process legitimate personal data.
- Never provide quote amounts outside the widget.
